title: ulmus × hollandica var. major
intro: Ulmus × hollandica var. major, commonly known as Field Elm, is a deciduous tree native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. The therapeutic actions of Field Elm include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory activities, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ulmus × hollandica var. major include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Ulmus × hollandica var. major has many benefits, such as providing air purification, reducing noise pollution, and stabilizing soil to prevent erosion. This plant also offers various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, improving respiratory health, and even helping to alleviate symptoms of allergies and asthma. Its shade can also provide relief from the heat, improve mood, and boost the immune system, while its roots can even help to absorb heavy metals from contaminated soil. By incorporating this versatile plant into our environment, we can enjoy cleaner air, improved physical and mental well-being, and a more sustainable and balanced ecosystem.
actions: Ulmus × hollandica var. major has many therapeutic actions, such as exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. Its therapeutic actions also include anti-cancer, anti-diabetic, and anti-hypertensive effects, making it a valuable tool in managing chronic diseases and promoting overall well-being. By reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, this plant can help people lead healthier, more active lives, free from the constraints of debilitating conditions. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage, promoting a longer, healthier life and improving daily functioning in individuals with chronic illnesses.
constituents: Ulmus × hollandica var. major has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, phenolic acids like ferulic and sinapic acid, and tannins like gallic and ellagic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making them useful in alleviating symptoms of various conditions, such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin infections. The flavonoids present in Ulmus × hollandica var. major can also help to improve cardiovascular health by reducing the risk of heart disease and lowering blood pressure, thereby enhancing overall well-being and quality of life. Furthermore, the antimicrobial properties of the plant's constituents can aid in wound healing and infection prevention, promoting a healthier and more resilient lifestyle.
parts: Ulmus × hollandica var. major has many medicinal parts, such as the bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and the leaves, which contain ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of Ulmus × hollandica var. major contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots of this plant are rich in isovaleric acid and other organic compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Additionally, the sap of Ulmus × hollandica var. major contains salicin and other phenolic compounds, which have been used to treat fever and pain.
side_effects: Ulmus × hollandica var. major can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or bark can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of tannins and other compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or extracts can lead to skin rashes, redness, and inflammation. In severe cases, improper use of Ulmus × hollandica var. major can also trigger asthma-like symptoms or interact with medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
